questions.question: An 'environmental portrait' is one where…
options: The subject is shown in a setting that tells you about them
Only the face fills the frame
The background is always pure white
questions.reviewHint: Environmental portraits include surroundings that say something about the subject.

questions.question: A 'headshot' portrait typically frames…
options: The head and shoulders, focusing on the face
The whole body in a scene
questions.reviewHint: Headshots concentrate on the face, head and shoulders.

questions.question: For flattering portraits with a softly blurred background, photographers often use…
options: A wide aperture (low f-number) for shallow depth of field
A very small aperture like f/22
questions.reviewHint: Wide apertures throw the background out of focus, isolating the subject.

questions.question: A popular portrait focal length on full frame is around…
questions.reviewHint: Short telephotos like 85mm flatter faces and give a comfortable working distance.

questions.question: Where should focus be placed in a portrait?
options: On the eyes (the nearest eye if the face is angled)
questions.reviewHint: Sharp eyes are critical in portraits; focus on the nearest eye when angled.

questions.question: Very wide-angle lenses are usually avoided for close head-and-shoulders portraits because they…
options: Can distort facial features unflatteringly up close
questions.reviewHint: Wide lenses up close exaggerate features, which is rarely flattering for faces.

questions.question: Soft, diffused light is popular for portraits because it…
options: Flatters skin and creates gentle shadows
questions.reviewHint: Soft light gives flattering, gentle transitions on skin.

questions.question: A 'lifestyle' portrait tends to be…
options: Candid and natural, showing the subject in real situations
Highly posed in a studio only
questions.reviewHint: Lifestyle portraits feel candid and natural rather than formally posed.

questions.question: Composition rules like the rule of thirds apply to portraits by…
options: Helping place the subject and eyes for a balanced frame
Forcing the subject to the centre
questions.reviewHint: Thirds, leading lines and framing all help compose a balanced portrait.

questions.question: Catchlights in a portrait subject's eyes are…
options: Small reflections of the light source that add life
questions.reviewHint: Catchlights are reflections in the eyes that make a portrait feel alive.

questions.question: A 'fine art' portrait is generally…
options: A more creative, expressive image driven by the photographer's vision
questions.reviewHint: Fine art portraits prioritise creative, expressive intent over documentation.
